title: Atalanta serrulata
taxonomy: Atalanta serrulata is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to the southeastern United States, specifically in the regions of Alabama, Georgia, and South Carolina. The plant is known for its showy purple flowers and is often found growing in dry to moist woodlands and along roadsides. Atalanta serrulata is also known as prairie clover or prairie bean, and is a member of the legume family. It is considered a rare species in some areas due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.
uses: Atalanta serrulata, also known as Atalantia monophylla, is a plant species native to tropical Asia. Atalanta serrulata has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
